tr.td: "cough" [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 2:19:46 PM]

section.title: MEDICAL EQUIPMENT
section.text: Patient has no history of implanted devices.

tr.td: Wegovy 0.25 mg/0.5 mL subcutaneous pen injector, [RxNorm: 2553501]
inject 0.25 mg SQ once a week X 12 wks
DISSOLVE ONE IN CHEEK EVERY 30MIN AS NEEDED FOR BREAKTHROUGH PAIN
Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily
Paxlovid 300 mg (150 mg x 2)-100 mg tablets in a dose pack
taek 3 tabs po bid for 5 days
DISSOLVE ONE IN CHEEK EVERY 30MIN AS NEEDED FOR BREAKTHROUGH PAIN
Take 1 tablet by mouth once daily

td.paragraph: PRESCRIBE: Ventolin HFA 90 mcg/actuation aerosol inhaler, 2 PUFFs Qid, # 108, RF: 2. (Transmitted by Gretchen Lockard, MD) (genric ok)
PRESCRIBE: Paxlovid 300 mg (150 mg x 2)-100 mg tablets in a dose pack, taek 3 tabs po bid for 5 days, # 30, RF: 0. (Transmitted by Gretchen Lockard, MD)

section.title: HEALTH CONCERNS
section.text: No health concerns information is available.

item.paragraph: Chief Complaint: "cough" [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 2:19:46 PM]

History of Present Illness: 1) Sick Visit: - Symptoms started: 1/2/26 no rash, yellow mucus - Myalgia (muscle pain, soreness): - Fatigue: - Fever/Chills: - Cough:yes - Sputum Production: - Ear Pain/Discomfort: - Nasal Congestion:no - Runny Nose:no - Sinus Pressure: slight pressure - Sore Throat: yes - Shortness of Breath: - Wheezing: - Sick Contacts: no, but went to house with 2 children - Vaccinated against COVID: - Boostered against COVID: - Vaccinated against Flu: -Lowered appetite [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 2:19:46 PM]
Past Medical History: chornic back pain, DM type 2, Hyperlipidemia [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 11/02/23 12:06 PM]
Family History: NA, adopted [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 11/02/23 12:06 PM]
Social History: [Tobacco: Never smoker] no EtOH No drujgs Married [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 11/02/23 12:06 PM]

Physical Examination: - General: fatigued, but nontoxic appearing, NAD - HEENT: sclera white. TM's normal retracted appearing. Nasal turbinates edematous, Posterior pharynx no erythema, no exudate. + sinus TTP -Neck: Shotty adenopathy - CV: regular rhythm, regular rate, no murmurs - Resp: thorax symmetric with good excursion,Ocasional expatory wheeze bilaterally - Neuro: alert, awake, normal speech -Covid Positive, Flu Negative [Updated by Gretchen Lockard, MD on 1/5/2026 2:19:46 PM]

Assessment & Plan: # COVID-19 (U07.1):Start paxlovid, patient was advised to stop lipitor while on paxlovid due to drug interactions # Wheeze (R06.2):Start albuterol Inhaler Qid # Acute sinusitis (J01.90):Likely due to covid # Hyperlipidemia due to type 2 diabetes mellitus (E11.69): patient immunocompromised
